description: Uniswap V2 is an automated market maker (AMM) DEX that uses constant-product (x*y=k) liquidity pools for permissionless token swaps on Ethereum. All 9 protocol contracts are immutable with no upgradeability. Governance (GovernorBravo with a 48-hour Timelock) exists but controls only the protocol fee switch via FeeToSetter and FeeTo — it cannot modify the core swap or liquidity logic. Total liquidity across all pairs is {{uniswapV2TotalLiquidity}}.

eth:0x1a9c8182c09f50c8318d769245bea52c32.name: Governance Timelock (48h)
eth:0x1a9c8182c09f50c8318d769245bea52c32.description: Timelock contract with a 48-hour minimum delay, controlled by GovernorBravo (eth:0x408E...24C3). Owns FeeToSetter and FeeTo, giving it the ability to change the protocol fee recipient address and toggle the 0.05% protocol fee on liquidity pools. Cannot modify swap logic, pool contracts, or the factory.

eth:0x408ed6354d4973f66138c91495f2f2fcbd.name: GovernorBravo Governance
eth:0x408ed6354d4973f66138c91495f2f2fcbd.description: On-chain governance contract (GovernorBravoDelegator) that proposes and votes on changes to the Timelock. UNI token holders vote on proposals that, if passed, are queued in the 48-hour Timelock. In practice, the only meaningful action available is controlling the protocol fee switch — all core contracts are immutable.

eth:0x5C69bEe701ef814a2B6a3EDD4B1652CB9c.name: UniswapV2Factory (All Pairs)
eth:0x5C69bEe701ef814a2B6a3EDD4B1652CB9c.description: Factory contract that has deployed {{uniswapV2PairCount}} liquidity pair contracts holding {{uniswapV2TotalLiquidity}} in aggregate liquidity. Each pair is an independent immutable contract holding reserves of two ERC-20 tokens. Liquidity is deposited by LPs and used for constant-product swaps.

codeAndAudits.title: Code & Audits
codeAndAudits.description: Smart contract analysis and audit history
